name: Buzludzha Monument
description: An abandoned saucer-shaped monument in the Bulgarian mountains.

name: Devil's Bridge
description: An architectural marvel that spans the Arda River, and has stories of a haunted past.

name: Belogradchik Rocks
description: Bizarrely shaped rock formations in northwest Bulgaria come with equally bizarre legends.

name: Monument to the Soviet Army
description: A Bulgarian monument to Russia's role in World War II is constantly being painted in pop culture protest.

name: Bulgarian State Monument
description: This massive concrete sculpture is said to be the heaviest communist monument on Earth.

name: Rila Monastery
description: This historic black and white monastery is one of Bulgaria's architectural jewels.

name: Historic Melnik
description: This tiny Bulgarian town offers a stunning location in the mountains and a storied history.

name: Museum of Socialist Art
description: A collection of art from Bulgaria's time as a communist state.

name: The Eyes of God
description: The ceiling of Prohodna Cave in northern Bulgaria affords a peek at the sky via two deity-sized-eye-shaped holes.

name: The Snail House
description: A flamboyant five-story building in Bulgaria resembles a crawling snail.

name: Alexander Nevsky Cathedral
description: A striking symbol of the Bulgarian capital.

name: Devil's Throat Cave
description: A spectacular Bulgarian cave resembling a devil's head with a waterfall rushing down its throat.

name: Perperikon
description: An ancient Thracian city where fire rituals were practiced on the largest cult altar in Southeast Europe.

name: Monument of the Bulgarian-Soviet Friendship
description: This once-grandiose behemoth of a monument from the socialist era now sits quiet and closed up.

name: Vidin Synagogue
description: The second-largest Jewish temple in Bulgaria was deserted after World War II, seized by the communist government and then abandoned again.

name: Thracian Tomb of Kazanlak
description: UNESCO-protected ancient burial ground.

name: Raketa Rakia
description: Eastern Bloc nostalgia, anybody?

name: Cape Kaliakra
description: The site of many battles and tragedies, including the 40 maidens who leapt into the sea.

name: Plovdiv Roman Theater
description: This ancient theater, restored in the 20th century, lives on as a venue for dramatic and musical performances.

name: Transfiguration Monastery
description: Outstanding, eerie frescoes and decorations fill this 19th-century monastery.

name: Perla 2
description: Not far from a popular beach, an unfinished, abandoned mansion for Bulgaria's last communist dictator.

name: The Necropolis of St. Sophia Church
description: Underneath this Byzantinian church lies a necropolis filled with ancient tombs and the ruins of previous churches.

name: National Palace of Culture
description: A vast Communist-era exhibition space in the Bulgarian capital.

name: Statue of Sveta Sofia
description: "Holy Wisdom" overlooks the city that became its namesake.

name: The Bells Monument
description: "Unity, Creativity, Beauty."

name: Chudnite Skali (Wonderful Rocks)
description: These natural cliffs look like they are a part of an old castle complex.

name: Pobiti Kamani Stones
description: Mysterious standing stone formations looks like a petrified forest.

name: Stone Mushrooms of Beli Plast
description: In a long-dry lake bed, these groups of massive stone pillars bear a striking resemblance to clumps of everyone's favorite fungi.

name: Alyosha Monument
description: A gigantic statue of a Russian soldier watches over the ancient city of Plovdiv, a prominent reminder of the Soviet period in Bulgaria.

name: Shipka Memorial Church
description: A magnificent, glittering building tucked into a tiny Bulgarian mountain town.

name: Shipka Pass
description: A mountain pass in Bulgaria's Balkan Mountains was the site of four historic battles.

name: The Red Church
description: Ruins of an early Roman Christian church dating back to the 4th to 5th centuries.

name: The Tomb of Seuthes III
description: The remarkably well-preserved final resting place of a powerful ancient Thracian king.

name: Krushuna Falls
description: These cascading falls resonate throughout the Bulgarian forest.

name: Transfiguration Monastery Tunnel
description: A mysterious tunnel hidden in the shadows of a colorful, historic sanctuary.

name: Defenders of Stara Zagora Memorial Complex
description: This massive stone and steel monument remembers a century-old battle that cost thousands of lives and nearly destroyed the city.

name: Arkutino School for Gifted Children
description: An unfinished complex built for children from around the world sits abandoned on the Black Sea coastline.

name: Rock Monastery of Shashkunite
description: Crossing a suspension bridge above a forest is the only way to reach this 12th-century monastery.

name: Madara Rider
description: Rock carving created 1,300 years ago 75 feet off the ground.

name: Retro Museum
description: The Grand Mall in Varna hosts one of the biggest collections of Soviet-era paraphernalia in Bulgaria.

name: The Largo
description: Sofia's legislative center is continuously reinventing itself.

name: Ascension Cathedral
description: Rebuilt several decades ago, its striking murals prevent it from being reconsecrated as a church.

name: Underground Mining Museum
description: One of the largest mines in Bulgaria is now home to a vast museum more than 100 feet underground.

name: Rose Museum
description: Said to be the only museum in the world dedicated to the most classic of all blooms, the rose.

name: Devetashka Cave
description: A massive cave once a secret military base is now home to thousands of bats.

name: Apartment of Soviet Life
description: This apartment is a true time capsule of local life in Bulgaria during the Soviet regime.

name: Surva Carnival at Pernik
description: Founded in the 4th-century, the town of Pernik plays host to the Surva Carnival⁠—a magnificent display of masked Kukeri dancers.

name: Asen Dynasty Monument
description: An homage to the medieval rulers of Bulgaria.

name: DZI Sofia Center
description: This building has two facades of clashing architectural designs.

name: Old Enio Bonchev Distillery & Family Museum
description: This rose and lavender oil distillery still operates as it did more than a century ago.

name: Gotse Delchev Temple
description: The temple that withstood the Nazis.

name: Troyan Monastery
description: At this 16th-century monastery in rural Bulgaria, visitors can also spend the night.

name: Water of Life Spring
description: A dragon-shaped fountain that is said to dispense healing water—unless you're evil.

name: Kaliakra Transmitter
description: Massive, unfinished broadcasting station stands as a monument to the fall of communism.

name: Palmatis Ruins
description: On the outskirts of the small village, an abandoned grassy field glitters thanks to the white stones of an ancient structure.

name: Lake Atanasovsko
description: One of the world’s only pink lakes where it's possible to take a dip.

name: Hagia Sophia Church
description: This fifth-century church is one of the oldest Christian sites in the Balkans.

name: The Red Flat
description: An interactive museum-like experience takes visitors back to 1980s Communist Bulgaria.

name: MiG-21 Monument
description: A supersonic jet stands on a pedestal in this rural village as a monument to military aviation.

name: Lisitsite Rope Bridge
description: At almost 900 feet long, this is Bulgaria's longest suspension bridge and the only way to reach the small village of Lisitsite.

name: Gorna Banya Abandoned Bath House
description: The famous springs of this small town still flow outside this historic building.

name: Abandoned Tsar Boris the Third Sanatorium
description: An abandoned sanatorium building from the early 20th century sits in the forest of the Rila mountains.

name: Royal Station Kazichene
description: The Bulgarian royal family exclusively used this ornate, now-abandoned train station.

name: Church of St. Stephen
description: Step inside this medieval Orthodox gem and travel back to the 16th century.

name: Mother Bulgaria Pantheon
description: While it would not look out of place among Serbian spomeniks, this massive Bulgarian monument commemorates one of their defeats.

name: Bankya Residence
description: Now a ruin, this grand resort was coveted by a communist autocrat and an eccentric Japanese golfer.

name: Roman Ruins Underneath a Starbucks
description: A chain coffee shop in Bulgaria that takes its Italian roots very seriously.

name: Abandoned Ski Jump
description: A crumbling monument to a now locally-extinct sport in Bulgaria's oldest ski resort.

name: Pantheon of National Revival Heroes
description: A Soviet-style monument, built after transferring the remains of heroes from their graves.

name: Nessebar Ethnographic Museum
description: Housed in a historic 1840 home, this museum provides a window into the lives of everyday, 18th- and 19th-century Bulgarians.

name: Dormition of the Mother of God Cathedral
description: Grand Orthodox cathedral with walls filled with frescoes and icons.

name: Abandoned Textile Factory
description: The social hall of this former factory is a Communist-era time capsule.

name: Interhotel Veliko Tarnovo
description: A decaying, yet still functioning, work of communist architecture.
